idea-plugin.name: DevGlobe
idea-plugin.description:
Appear on a 3D globe in real time while you code. Your activity shows up live on devglobe.xyz — other developers can see you, discover your projects, and find your links.

How it works

  1. Sign in on devglobe.xyz with GitHub
  2. Copy your API key from settings
  3. Open the DevGlobe tool window in your IDE and paste the key
  4. You appear on the globe

The extension sends a heartbeat every 30 seconds while you code. It pauses automatically after 1 minute of inactivity. After 10 minutes without activity, you disappear from the globe.

Features

  • Live heartbeat — your presence on the globe in real time
  • Language detection — uses your IDE's native FileType system, supports all languages
  • Git integration — detects your repo and tracks insertions/deletions over 24h
  • Status message — tell others what you're working on
  • Repo sharing — you decide whether your repo name is visible (off by default)
  • Status bar — your coding time today displayed live
  • Notifications — native IDE notifications for every action
  • Offline recovery — automatically resumes when the connection is back

What DevGlobe gives you

  • Visibility — your GitHub, X, projects, and links accessible to all devs on the globe
  • Network — discover who's coding right now, in which language, and find their links
  • Motivation — weekly leaderboard by coding time + daily streak
  • Project showcase — highlight up to 10 projects on the globe

Privacy

This plugin is open source. Your source code, file contents, keystrokes, and IP address are never sent. Your location is approximate (~11 km). Your repo name is only visible if you explicitly enable it. Your API key is stored in your IDE's native credential manager (PasswordSafe).

Compatible with all JetBrains IDEs: IntelliJ IDEA, WebStorm, PyCharm, GoLand, Rider, PhpStorm, CLion, RubyMine, DataGrip, Android Studio.
